course_title: Midwifery Care across the Pregnancy Continuum

learning_outcomes.description: Interpret the frameworks and evidence which can be used to inform midwifery practice for preconception, antenatal, intrapartum and postnatal care.

Identify the role of the midwife in providing culturally responsive care from the preconception period through the pregnancy continuum to women, babies and families, including those undergoing assisted reproduction.

Explain the legal and professional responsibilities of the midwife for safe drug administration during pregnancy, labour, and birth, including knowledge of scope of practice and the need to accept accountability and responsibility for their own actions.

Examining the aims of pre-pregnancy care discussing safety and quality for women and families, while exploring the meaning of fertility and infertility and identify the key aspects of assisted reproduction.
Reflect on the philosophy of midwifery led care, and importance of midwifery partnership to midwifery practice and womens experiences of childbirth, particularly those who are vulnerable.

assessments.assessment_title: Fact Sheet and Individual Oral Presentation
assessments.due_date:
Week 4 Mon - Week 12 Fri

Students will choose a week to present in the first IBL class in Week 2. Students will present their Fact Sheet individually in Weeks 4, 6, 8, 10 and 12.
assessments.task_description: Assessment Item 1: Students are to prepare a Fact Sheet on a variety of topics that address preconception, antenatal, intrapartum or postnatal care. Students are then to present a 3-minute presentation outlining the information in their Fact Sheet. Please refer to the marking criteria and task description within the course BlackBoard site for further details.
assessments.submission_guidelines: All courses require students to submit an electronic version of their assignment via BlackBoard, via the Assessment 1 link. Please check the companion BlackBoard assessment area for specific instructions on this process.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The aim of this assignment is to increase students' knowledge of the role of continuity of midwifery care in supporting women with additional complexities. The completion of the assignment will provide students with an overview of the benefits and challenges of working in partnership with diverse groups of women and their families.
assessments.submission_guidelines: All courses require students to submit an electronic version of their assignment via BlackBoard, via the Assessment 2 link. Please check the companion BlackBoard assessment area for specific instructions on this process.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The examination content and questions will be taken from course lectures, learning modules and readings.

Further information regarding the examination will be provided throughout the semester.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The examination will be through the Inspera platform and will be held online, on campus, and will be invigilated.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Online Learning Modules (independent study)

You are expected to spend seven (7) hours per week on learning for this course. This includes working through the module materials provided and preparation for your IBL workshops. Resources can be found in companion BlackBoard site.
